commit.message:
feat(multiplayer): rejoin-or-leave UX for active room conflicts

When createRoom() or joinRoom() returns a CONFLICT because the user
already has an active room, include the blocking room's ID in the error
payload (details.activeRoomId) so the client can offer a recovery path.

LobbyEntryDialog now detects this conflict and replaces the plain error
text with an inline prompt offering two options:
- Rejoin: navigates directly to /play/lobby/{activeRoomId}
- Leave & Continue: calls leaveRoom(), then retries the original action

Error handling: if leaveRoom fails, the error message is shown and the
dialog stays open.

AppError gains a dedicated CONFLICT union member with optional
details?: ConflictDetails. The appError() factory is updated with
typed overloads to preserve type safety at call sites.

Closes Story 17.3 — final story of EPIC 17.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace TUI onboarding with Flutter API key form

- Replace onboarding_screen.dart: was running 'ironclaw onboard' TUI
  which does NOT write API keys (only detects env vars and warns).
  Now a clean Flutter form: provider picker, API key field (with
  show/hide toggle), model dropdown with custom option, Save button.
  Supports isFirstRun flow (navigate to dashboard) or edit flow (pop).

- Delete configure_screen.dart: was a redundant copy of onboarding_screen
  also running 'ironclaw onboard'. Removed entirely.

- Update dashboard_screen.dart: remove 'Onboarding' and 'Configure'
  cards (both ran ironclaw onboard TUI). Single 'AI Providers' card
  routes to ProvidersScreen for all provider/key management.

- Fix provider_config_service.dart: readConfig() now also reads
  /root/.ironclaw/.env to detect which providers have API keys set,
  returning a 'providers' map so the UI shows configured status.

- Fix provider_detail_screen.dart: replace existingApiKey param with
  isConfigured bool. API key field always starts empty for security;
  helper text says 'Key already set — enter new key to replace it'
  when configured. Remove button shown when isConfigured is true.

- Fix providers_screen.dart: read config['providers'] correctly from
  updated readConfig(), pass isConfigured to ProviderDetailScreen.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: overlay menu rendering and border sizing

- Wrap normal CRT rendering in if !paused && !quick_overlay guard
  to prevent it from overwriting pause/overlay composite_buffer output
- Widen quick overlay box from 20 to 24 tiles to prevent text overflow
- Remove debug logging and diagnostic keyboard shortcuts
- Add parallelism regression tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(jetson): resolve critical CUDA threading, crash bugs, and edge cases

C1+C2: Replace direct TensorRTInference with InferenceWorkerPool in
gui_app.py — engines now load on dedicated CUDA thread, eliminating
context-affinity violations and shared-buffer race conditions.

C3: Fix TypeError crash in provision.py when device_id is int
(str(device_id)[:8] instead of device_id[:8]).

C4: Remove conflicting WatchdogSec=120 from static agrikd.service
template (setup_jetson.sh generates the actual Docker-based service).

H1: Defer gui_app.py file logging setup until after os.chdir() so
log files are created in the correct directory.

H2: Add basic config validation in gui_app.py main().

H3: Wrap sync_engine.py resp.json() in try/except to prevent crash
on non-JSON responses (e.g., proxy error pages).

H4: Add max_retries=5 to camera._open_with_reconnect() to prevent
infinite blocking during shutdown.

H5: Call pool.shutdown() in gui_app.py closeEvent to release CUDA
device memory on GUI close.

H7: Run health_server.py DB cleanup in background thread to avoid
blocking the Waitress request thread.

M9: Throttle gui_app.py cleanup to every 50 predictions (was every 1).
M10: Guard sync_engine.py device_id int cast with try/except.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: static block context isolation, continue target validation, object literal semicolons, break/continue ASI

- Static blocks: save/clear loop_stack and try_finally_stack in compiler so break/continue from outer loops don't leak through
- Continue statement: validate that labeled continue targets iteration statements, not arbitrary labeled blocks
- LoopContext: add is_iteration flag to distinguish iteration statements from labeled blocks/switches
- Object literal parser: stop skipping semicolons in property loop (semicolons are invalid in object literals)
- Break/continue ASI: skip LineTerminators before checking for semicolon terminator
- Static block parser: save/clear generator and function context, push method context for super access
- Arrow function body: clear forbidden_await_identifier (static block sets it, arrow body clears it)
- Class name await: check forbid_await_identifier without in_await_context exception for module mode
- Variable declaration: reject await as binding name when forbidden
- Computed property names in class: wrap with with_allowed_in to prevent FORBID_IN leak
- Method/constructor context: push before params for super in default parameter expressions
- Module mode: wrap parsing with forbidden_await_identifier
- Remove dead code: unused destructuring_element_scan_mask and params_scan_mask functions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: step 9 tries pre-built .engine before ONNX fallback

Download priority for each model:
1. Try pre-built .engine from Supabase Storage matching exact
   device tag (e.g. jetson-orin-nano_trt10.3) — instant, no convert
2. If no engine found, download ONNX → convert in step 10 (~10 min)

This avoids re-converting ONNX on every fresh setup when a matching
.engine was previously uploaded by the same device type.

commit.message:
feat(multiplayer): add abandon_stale_rooms RPC and cleanup on room create/join

Story 17.2 - On-Demand Stale Room Cleanup

- Add abandon_stale_rooms Postgres RPC (SECURITY DEFINER) that marks
  all lobby/playing rooms owned by the caller as abandoned when their
  updated_at is older than stale_threshold_hours (default 2h), and
  removes the caller's room_players rows for those rooms.
- Add cleanupStaleRooms() helper in actionHelpers.ts that wraps the
  RPC; errors are logged but never block the caller.
- Call cleanupStaleRooms() in createRoom() and joinRoom() before
  findActiveRoomId() so stale rooms are cleared automatically.
- Add abandon_stale_rooms to Supabase TypeScript types.
- Extend actions.test.ts with rpc mock + tests verifying cleanup is
  called before active-room check and that RPC failure is non-blocking.

deps: upgrade ureq 2→3 and toml 1.1.1→1.1.2

- Bump ureq from 2.12.1 to 3.3.0 (Dependabot PR #20)
- Bump toml from 1.1.1+spec-1.1.0 to 1.1.2+spec-1.1.0 (Dependabot PR #19)

ureq 3.x is a ground-up rewrite; API migration:
- AgentBuilder::new().timeout() → Agent::config_builder().timeout_global()
- .set(header, value) → .header(header, value)
- .timeout() on request → per-request .config().timeout_global().build()
- Error::Status(code, response) → http_status_as_error(false) + Ok(response) branch
- Error::Transport(e) → wildcard Err(e) arm
- response.into_json() → response.body_mut().read_json()
- response.into_string() → response.body_mut().read_to_string()
- response.into_reader() → response.body_mut().as_reader()
- response.status() → response.status().as_u16()
- response.header(name) → response.headers().get(name)

All 390 tests pass.
